report_name: ADT Confirms Data Breach by ShinyHunters After Vishing Attack Compromises Okta and Salesforce

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A data extortion group that compromised an ADT employee's Okta credentials via a vishing attack to access the company's Salesforce instance and exfiltrate customer data. The group threatened to leak 10 million records if a ransom was not paid.
threat_actors.goals: To extort a ransom payment from ADT by threatening to leak stolen customer data.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Voice
attack_patterns.description: Used as the initial access vector, where attackers manipulated an ADT employee over the phone (vishing) to obtain their Okta SSO credentials.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Used valid employee credentials obtained through vishing to gain authenticated access to ADT's cloud environment via the Okta SSO platform, which helped evade immediate detection.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The attackers targeted ADT's Salesforce environment to query and collect sensitive customer and prospective customer information (PII).

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The collected customer data was exfiltrated from the Salesforce cloud environment to attacker-controlled infrastructure, likely using standard HTTPS protocols to blend in with normal traffic.

identities.name: ADT Inc.
identities.description: A leading home security provider that suffered a data breach after an employee's Okta credentials were compromised, leading to the exfiltration of customer PII from its Salesforce instance.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Exfiltration Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure controlled by ShinyHunters to receive the customer PII exfiltrated from ADT's Salesforce environment.
